title: Ceropteris triangularis
taxonomy: Ceropteris triangularis is classified as a species within the genus Ceropteris. This genus is part of the fern family Ceropteridaceae. Ceropteridaceae is a family of ferns in the order Cyatheales. The order Cyatheales is a group of tree ferns that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Ceropteris triangularis reflects its evolutionary relationships and characteristics within the plant kingdom.

intro: Ceropteris triangularis, also known as the Pacific sword fern, is a species of fern native to the Pacific coast of North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and boost the immune system.
